text:
The task targets two mechanisms: (1) series limit enforcement and (2) out-of-bound/out-of-order sample handling in the TSDB head.

**Series limit enforcement**: `tsdb/head.go` defines `SeriesLifecycleCallback` (line 234) with `PreCreation()` — called in `getOrCreateWithOptionalID()` (line 1845) before any new series is added. A non-nil error from `PreCreation` prevents series creation, which is how cardinality/series limits are enforced. The `numSeries` atomic counter (line 70) tracks the active series count.

**Out-of-bound / out-of-order sample handling**: The core logic lives in `tsdb/head_append.go`. The `memSeries.appendable()` (line 643), `appendableHistogram()` (line 688), and `appendableFloatHistogram()` (line 730) methods determine whether a sample is in-order, OOO-eligible, or out-of-bounds by comparing against `headMaxt`, `minValidTime`, and `oooTimeWindow`. They return `storage.ErrOutOfBounds` or `storage.ErrOutOfOrderSample` (defined in `storage/interface.go`, lines 34-38). The `handleAppendableError()` function (line 1275) dispatches these errors to the appropriate rejection counters. The `outOfBoundSamples` metric counter is defined in `tsdb/head.go` (line 478). `tsdb/head_append_v2.go` is the v2 append path with the same error handling pattern.
